task_completion.description: Agent used Google Admin Toolbox Dig to check DNS A records.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the browser navigated to Google Admin Toolbox Dig (toolbox.googleapps.com) with queries for google.com, amazon.com, and microsoft.com.

task_completion.description: Agent used SSL Shopper or DigiCert SSL Checker to check SSL certificates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the browser navigated to SSL Shopper (sslshopper.com) or DigiCert SSL Checker (digicert.com) with queries for the three target domains.

task_completion.description: Agent created a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Incident 2411 Audit'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox bookmarks menu, toolbar, or library shows a folder explicitly named 'Incident 2411 Audit'.

task_completion.description: Agent bookmarked the DNS and SSL result pages into the 'Incident 2411 Audit' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the 'Incident 2411 Audit' bookmark folder are visible and contain links to the specific result pages for the DNS and SSL checks of the three domains.

task_completion.description: Agent created the audit_log.json file in the ~/Documents/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file manager shows a file named 'audit_log.json' located in the 'Documents' folder of the user's home directory.

task_completion.description: The JSON file contains the correct structure and audited data for all three domains.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The open 'audit_log.json' file shows a valid JSON structure with top-level keys 'google.com', 'amazon.com', and 'microsoft.com'. Each key contains 'ip_address', 'ssl_issuer', and 'ssl_expiry' (in YYYY-MM-DD format) populated with values matching the tool outputs.

integrity.description: Agent used the Firefox GUI to perform the checks and create bookmarks, rather than bypassing the browser with terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show actual Firefox browser windows open to the specified web tools and the Firefox bookmarking interface being used, not just terminal outputs of 'dig' or 'openssl'.

integrity.description: The recorded JSON data comes from the actual web tools, not hallucinated or copied directly from a prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values entered into the JSON file should correspond to the visual outputs seen on the Google Admin Toolbox and SSL checker web pages in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The JSON file was genuinely created on the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON content is visible in a real text editor (like gedit, nano, vim) or a terminal `cat` command showing the file path, rather than just being printed in the agent's internal thought process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected values (may vary slightly by region/time but should be close): Google.com - IP: ~142.251.111.139, Issuer: Google Trust Services, Expiry: ~2026-04-27. Amazon.com - IP: ~98.87.170.71, Issuer: DigiCert Inc, Expiry: ~2027-01-23. Microsoft.com - IP: ~13.107.246.40, Issuer: Microsoft Corporation, Expiry: ~2026-08-03. Required tools: Google Admin Toolbox Dig (toolbox.googleapps.com) for DNS, and either SSL Shopper (sslshopper.com) or DigiCert SSL Checker (digicert.com) for SSL.
